final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.label: Final Capstone: Build the Resource Guide History
final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.summary: Build a four-commit neighborhood resource guide history, make real file edits, recover from an accidental staged change, and prove the final handoff.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `resource-guide/` repository.

Initialize `resource-guide/` on `main`, set the repository-only learner identity, stage the provided starting files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: The folder still needs its own repository metadata. Keep the first branch name in mind.

help.concept: The capstone begins with the same honest sequence as the course: initialize, identify, stage, commit, verify.
help.hint_1: Make sure you are already inside the project folder before initializing it.
help.hint_2: Finish by asking Git which branch is currently checked out.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md pages/
git commit -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"
git status --short
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `resource-guide/` repository.

Initialize `resource-guide/` on `main`, set the repository-only learner identity, stage the provided starting files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf resource-guide
mkdir -p resource-guide/pages
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' > resource-guide/README.md
printf '%s\n' 'Help desk closes at 6 PM.' > resource-guide/pages/contact.md
printf '%s\n' 'Welcome to the neighborhood guide.' > resource-guide/pages/welcome.md
printf '%s\n' 'Temporary launch note.' > resource-guide/pages/old-note.md

printf '%s\n' 'Welcome to the neighborhood guide.' > resource-guide/pages/welcome.md
printf '%s\n' 'Temporary launch note.' > resource-guide/pages/old-note.md

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Write the Services Page
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
The first resource-guide snapshot is already committed. Open `pages/services.md` in the editor and replace the placeholder with these three lines:

```text
- Food pantry
- Health clinic
- Job center
```

Save the file, then use Terminal to review its state, stage only this page, and commit it as `Add local services page`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Choose only the paths that belong to this checkpoint.

help.concept: A new page earns its own focused snapshot because it is a complete, reviewable addition.
help.hint_1: Do not broaden the staging boundary just to make status quieter.
help.hint_2: Review the staged paths or patch before moving on.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode: <!-- Replace with the local services list -->
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
printf "%s\n" "- Food pantry" "- Health clinic" "- Job center" > pages/services.md
git status --short
git add pages/services.md
git commit -m "Add local services page"
git status --short
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
The first resource-guide snapshot is already committed. Open `pages/services.md` in the editor and replace the placeholder with these three lines:

```text
- Food pantry

```

Save the file, then use Terminal to review its state, stage only this page, and commit it as `Add local services page`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf resource-guide
mkdir -p resource-guide/pages
git -C resource-guide init -q -b main
git -C resource-guide config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C resource-guide config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' > resource-guide/README.md
printf '%s\n' 'Help desk closes at 6 PM.' > resource-guide/pages/contact.md
printf '%s\n' 'Welcome to the neighborhood guide.' > resource-guide/pages/welcome.md
printf '%s\n' 'Temporary launch note.' > resource-guide/pages/old-note.md
git -C resource-guide add -A
git -C resource-guide commit -q -m 'Start neighborhood resource guide'

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Edit the Help Desk Hours
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
The services page is committed. Open `pages/contact.md` in the editor and change the closing time from `6 PM` to `5 PM`.

Save the file, inspect the unstaged patch, stage only the contact page, and commit it as `Update help desk hours`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Choose only the paths that belong to this checkpoint.

help.concept: Inspecting before staging keeps a small correction precise and easy to review.
help.hint_1: Do not broaden the staging boundary just to make status quieter.
help.hint_2: Review the staged paths or patch before moving on.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
printf "%s\n" "Help desk closes at 5 PM." > pages/contact.md
git diff -- pages/contact.md
git add pages/contact.md
git commit -m "Update help desk hours"
git status --short
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
The services page is committed. Open `pages/contact.md` in the editor and change the closing time from `6 PM` to `5 PM`.

Save the file, inspect the unstaged patch, stage only the contact page, and commit it as `Update help desk hours`.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Add the Ignore Policy
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
The contact-page update is committed, and `generated/preview.html` is local build output. Open `.gitignore` in the editor and add one line: `generated/`.

Save the policy, then use Terminal to stage only `.gitignore`, verify that the preview is ignored, and review the staged path list. Do not commit yet.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Start in the editor and make only the content change described in the task.

help.concept: The ignore policy is source-controlled, while generated preview output stays outside history.
help.hint_1: Keep the file path and exact requested text in view.
help.hint_2: Switch to the terminal only after the saved file matches the scenario.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
printf "generated/\n" > .gitignore
git add .gitignore
git check-ignore generated/preview.html
git diff --staged --name-only
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
The contact-page update is committed, and `generated/preview.html` is local build output. Open `.gitignore` in the editor and add one line: `generated/`.

Save the policy, then use Terminal to stage only `.gitignore`, verify that the preview is ignored, and review the staged path list. Do not commit yet.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Recover from the Accidental README Edit
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
The ignore policy is staged, but an accidental README edit was staged with it. Correct that mistake without rewriting history: remove the README change from the proposed snapshot and return the working file to its recorded version.

Then rename `pages/welcome.md` to `pages/getting-help.md`, remove `pages/old-note.md`, and review the complete staged structural cleanup. Do not commit yet.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Move the tracked path through Git so the structural change is staged.

help.concept: Related cleanup actions can share one final snapshot when they serve the same handoff goal.
help.hint_1: Confirm that the new path exists and the old path no longer appears as tracked.
help.hint_2: Review the staged name-status summary before committing.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git restore --staged README.md
git restore README.md
git mv pages/welcome.md pages/getting-help.md
git rm pages/old-note.md
git diff --staged --name-status
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
The ignore policy is staged, but an accidental README edit was staged with it. Correct that mistake without rewriting history: remove the README change from the proposed snapshot and return the working file to its recorded version.

Then rename `pages/welcome.md` to `pages/getting-help.md`, remove `pages/old-note.md`, and review the complete staged structural cleanup. Do not commit yet.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Commit and Audit the Final Handoff
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `resource-guide/` repository.

Commit the staged cleanup as `Prepare resource guide handoff`, then audit the one-line history, latest snapshot, tracked files, ignore rule, and clean status.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Record only the staged snapshot and use the message requested by the scenario.

help.concept: A production-ready handoff is proven by its history, current tracked files, ignore behavior, and clean working state.
help.hint_1: The message should describe the project result, not the commands you used.
help.hint_2: After recording it, inspect history and any work that remains.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Prepare resource guide handoff"
git log --oneline
git show --stat HEAD
git ls-files
git check-ignore generated/preview.html
git status --short
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `resource-guide/` repository.

Commit the staged cleanup as `Prepare resource guide handoff`, then audit the one-line history, latest snapshot, tracked files, ignore rule, and clean status.

finalCapstone.description:
Build a complete local history for the neighborhood resource guide. The six milestones are cumulative: create the baseline, edit and commit two focused content changes, add an ignore policy, recover from an accidental staged README edit, perform the final rename and removal, and audit the four-commit handoff.

The final repository keeps `README.md`, replaces the welcome page with `pages/getting-help.md`, removes `pages/old-note.md`, ignores generated previews, and ends clean.

intro.title: Turn a Working Folder into a Trustworthy Handoff
intro.bodyMarkdown:
A neighborhood resource guide has useful starting content, an obsolete note, and a generated preview that should never enter history. Before another maintainer receives it, the project needs a timeline that explains how the guide became ready.

Across six cumulative milestones, you will initialize the repository, create its baseline, edit and commit two focused content changes, add an ignore policy, recover from an accidental staged README edit, rename the welcome page, remove the obsolete note, and verify the final snapshot.

Nothing is uploaded. The result is a local four-commit repository whose current files, ignore behavior, and clean status all agree with the story in its history.
